title: Separate liveness and readiness probes for DB health
description:
# GitHub Issue #163: Separate liveness and readiness probes to prevent DB-induced CrashLoop

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Both the liveness and readiness probes on the `keystone-api` container currently use the same check: `HTTPGet /v3:5000`. This endpoint requires a working database connection — if MariaDB is temporarily unavailable, `/v3` returns HTTP 500, the liveness probe fails, and kubelet kills the container. With multiple replicas this creates a thundering-herd restart: all pods crash-loop simultaneously and bombard the recovering database with connection storms, delaying recovery further.

Separate the probes by intent:

- **Liveness probe** → `TCPSocket` on port 5000. This verifies that the uWSGI process is alive and accepting connections. A TCP check does not exercise the database path, so a transient DB outage will not trigger unnecessary restarts.
- **Readiness probe** → `HTTPGet /v3` on port 5000 (unchanged). This verifies full API functionality including database connectivity. When the DB is down, the pod becomes not-ready and is removed from the Service endpoints — no traffic is routed to it, but the process stays alive and can recover in-place once the DB returns.

```go
LivenessProbe: &corev1.Probe{
    ProbeHandler: corev1.ProbeHandler{
        TCPSocket: &corev1.TCPSocketAction{
            Port: intstr.FromInt32(5000),
        },
    },
    InitialDelaySeconds: 15,
    PeriodSeconds:       20,
},
ReadinessProbe: &corev1.Probe{
    ProbeHandler: corev1.ProbeHandler{
        HTTPGet: &corev1.HTTPGetAction{
            Path: "/v3",
            Port: intstr.FromInt32(5000),
        },
    },
    InitialDelaySeconds: 5,
    PeriodSeconds:       10,
},
```

**Motivation**: A shared database is a common-mode failure — restarting the application pod cannot fix it. The established pattern (proven by operators like Keycloak/Quarkus) is: liveness checks the process, readiness checks the function. When the DB recovers, pods that remained alive immediately pass their readiness probe and re-enter the Service endpoints without a cold start. This reduces mean-time-to-recovery from minutes (CrashLoop backoff) to seconds (next readiness probe cycle).

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` (`buildKeystoneDeployment`, lines 170-189)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Liveness probe uses `TCPSocket` on port 5000
- [ ] Readiness probe uses `HTTPGet /v3` on port 5000 (unchanged)
- [ ] Pods remain running (no restart) when MariaDB is temporarily unavailable
- [ ] Pods are removed from Service endpoints when MariaDB is unavailable (readiness fails)
- [ ] Pods re-enter Service endpoints within one readiness probe period after DB recovery
- [ ] Unit tests verify probe types and configuration values
- [ ] Existing liveness/readiness timing parameters (initialDelaySeconds, periodSeconds) remain unchanged

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/163

stories.title: Keystone pods survive transient DB outages without restarting
stories.want: Keystone API pods to remain running when MariaDB is temporarily unavailable
stories.so_that: the pods can recover in-place once the database returns, avoiding CrashLoop backoff delays and thundering-herd restarts
criteria: Liveness probe uses TCPSocket on port 5000 instead of HTTPGet /v3
Pods are not killed by kubelet when MariaDB is temporarily unavailable
Pods re-enter Service endpoints within one readiness probe period (10s) after DB recovery
No change to readiness probe — it still uses HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000

stories.title: Keystone pods are removed from Service endpoints when DB is down
stories.want: Keystone API pods to be removed from Service endpoints when they cannot serve requests due to DB unavailability
stories.so_that: no user traffic is routed to pods that would return HTTP 500 errors
criteria: Readiness probe uses HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000 (unchanged)
Readiness probe fails when MariaDB is unavailable (HTTP 500 from /v3)
Pod is removed from Service endpoints when readiness probe fails
Readiness probe initialDelaySeconds=5 and periodSeconds=10 remain unchanged

stories.title: Liveness probe verifies uWSGI process health without DB dependency
stories.want: the liveness probe to only check that the uWSGI process is alive and accepting TCP connections
stories.so_that: a common-mode DB failure does not cascade into unnecessary pod restarts across all replicas
criteria: Liveness probe uses TCPSocket action targeting port 5000
Liveness probe does not exercise any database code path
Liveness probe initialDelaySeconds=15 and periodSeconds=20 remain unchanged
Liveness probe correctly detects a crashed uWSGI process (TCP connection refused)

stories.title: Unit tests verify separated probe configuration
stories.want: unit tests to assert the correct probe types and timing parameters
stories.so_that: regressions to the old shared-probe behavior are caught immediately in CI
criteria: Unit test asserts liveness probe uses TCPSocket (not HTTPGet)
Unit test asserts liveness probe TCPSocket targets port 5000
Unit test asserts readiness probe uses HTTPGet with path /v3 on port 5000
Unit test asserts both probes retain their original timing parameters

stories.title: Documentation reflects separated probe design
stories.want: the keystone-reconciler reference documentation to accurately describe the separated probe configuration
stories.so_that: other engineers understand the intentional probe separation and its rationale
criteria: Probes table in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md shows Liveness as TCPSocket on port 5000
Probes table shows Readiness as HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000 (unchanged)
Documentation explains the rationale for probe separation

requirements.description: The keystone-api container liveness probe SHALL use TCPSocket on port 5000
requirements.rationale: A TCP check verifies the uWSGI process is alive without exercising the database path, preventing unnecessary pod restarts during transient DB outages

scenarios.name: Liveness probe uses TCPSocket
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container LivenessProbe.ProbeHandler.TCPSocket is set with Port 5000

scenarios.name: Liveness probe does not use HTTPGet
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container LivenessProbe.ProbeHandler.HTTPGet is nil

requirements.description: The keystone-api container readiness probe SHALL continue using HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000
requirements.rationale: The readiness probe must exercise the full API stack including database connectivity to ensure pods only receive traffic when fully functional

scenarios.name: Readiness probe uses HTTPGet
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container ReadinessProbe.ProbeHandler.HTTPGet is set with Path /v3 and Port 5000

scenarios.name: Readiness probe port matches container port

requirements.description: The liveness probe timing parameters SHALL remain unchanged (initialDelaySeconds=15, periodSeconds=20)
requirements.rationale: Existing timing parameters are tuned for the Keystone startup characteristics and must not be altered

requirements.description: The keystone-reconciler reference documentation SHALL reflect the updated probe configuration
requirements.rationale: Documentation must be kept in sync with implementation to prevent confusion

scenarios.name: Probes table shows TCPSocket for liveness
scenarios.then: the Probes table shows Liveness as TCPSocket on port 5000 (no path)
and_then: the table explains the rationale for using TCPSocket

scenarios.name: Probes table shows HTTPGet for readiness
scenarios.then: the Probes table shows Readiness as HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000 (unchanged)

scenarios.name: Documentation includes rationale
scenarios.when: a developer reads the Probes section
scenarios.then: the documentation explains why liveness uses TCPSocket (avoid DB-induced restarts)

tasks.title: Change liveness probe from HTTPGet to TCPSocket in buildKeystoneDeployment and update unit tests (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`, modify the `buildKeystoneDeployment` function (lines 185-194) to replace the liveness probe HTTPGet handler with a TCPSocket handler on port 5000. Keep InitialDelaySeconds=15 and PeriodSeconds=20. Leave the readiness probe (lines 195-204) unchanged. Then in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`, update the liveness probe assertions in `TestReconcileDeployment_DeploymentSpec` (lines 237-243) to assert TCPSocket instead of HTTPGet: verify TCPSocket is not nil, TCPSocket.Port is 5000, and HTTPGet is nil. Readiness probe assertions (lines 245-251) remain unchanged.

tasks.title: Update keystone-reconciler reference documentation to reflect separated probe design (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`, update the Probes table (around line 600-603) to reflect the new liveness probe type. Change the Liveness row from `| Liveness | /v3 | 5000 | 15s | 20s |` to `| Liveness | TCPSocket | 5000 | 15s | 20s |` (or adjust the table header to include a Type column). Add a brief note below the table explaining the rationale: liveness uses TCPSocket to avoid DB-induced restarts; readiness uses HTTPGet /v3 to verify full API functionality including database connectivity.

test_specifications.story: Unit tests verify separated probe configuration
test_specifications.expected: LivenessProbe.TCPSocket is not nil, TCPSocket.Port equals 5000, LivenessProbe.HTTPGet is nil, InitialDelaySeconds=15, PeriodSeconds=20

test_specifications.story: Unit tests verify separated probe configuration
test_specifications.expected: ReadinessProbe.HTTPGet is not nil, HTTPGet.Path equals /v3, HTTPGet.Port equals 5000, InitialDelaySeconds=5, PeriodSeconds=10 — all unchanged

test_specifications.story: Unit tests verify separated probe configuration
test_specifications.expected: Liveness InitialDelaySeconds=15 and PeriodSeconds=20; Readiness InitialDelaySeconds=5 and PeriodSeconds=10

test_specifications.story: Liveness probe verifies uWSGI process health without DB dependency
test_specifications.expected: LivenessProbe.HTTPGet is nil — verifies no HTTP handler is configured on liveness

test_specifications.story: Keystone pods survive transient DB outages without restarting
test_specifications.expected: Full reconciliation flow succeeds with the updated probe configuration — integration test does not assert probe types directly but validates end-to-end reconciliation

review_criteria: Liveness probe uses TCPSocket on port 5000 (not HTTPGet) in buildKeystoneDeployment
Readiness probe remains unchanged: HTTPGet /v3 on port 5000
Liveness timing parameters unchanged: initialDelaySeconds=15, periodSeconds=20
Readiness timing parameters unchanged: initialDelaySeconds=5, periodSeconds=10
Unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go assert TCPSocket for liveness and HTTPGet for readiness
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Probes table updated to reflect TCPSocket liveness
golangci-lint passes with no new warnings
go vet passes with no errors
implementation_notes:
Minimal change: replace HTTPGet with TCPSocket in the liveness probe handler within buildKeystoneDeployment. The corev1.TCPSocketAction type is already available via the corev1 import. intstr.FromInt32 is already used for the readiness probe port. No new dependencies or imports required.

The change affects exactly 2 production lines (the ProbeHandler block for liveness) and ~6 test assertion lines. Documentation update is a table row change plus a rationale note.

Pattern followed: existing probe configuration pattern in the same function, just switching the handler type.
